what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in the dragnet-development channel that records user feedback about a duplicated and truncated heading or title at the top of a section.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development
It links to board://item-8ec1cd23 via commented_on and to an image attachment via image://dragnet-development/comment/item-2175d733
History shows the item was filed and then its links were edited in place
The markdown body contains a [section] marker followed by informal UX feedback
The feedback states two explicit rules: nothing should be truncated, and there should be no duplicate headings/titles
standouts: The note frames the issue as a design invariant rather than a one-off bug
It links both to a prior board item and to a screenshot image
It was authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, state, links, history, and timestamps
Uses a markdown body with a bracketed section marker
History records both filing and an in-place link edit
concerns: The second history entry has an empty 'by' field for the link edit
The feedback is informal and lacks a specific component or location identifier
notes: Tim posted a UX observation in dragnet-development that a section heading is duplicated and truncated, linking to a prior board item and an image.
questions: Which specific page or component shows the duplicated/truncated heading?
What is the content of the linked prior item board://item-8ec1cd23?
Does the attached image show the duplicated heading?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is a feedback note, and the file records UX feedback plus links to related context.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to report a UI polish issue and establish the design rule that section headings must never repeat or be clipped.
novelty: It frames a UI defect as a journal-side principle or invariant rather than a conventional bug report.
contribution: It contributes a concise UX rule statement and contextual links to a prior board item and an image, preserving the feedback for the relevant section UI.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel reports that a section heading or title is duplicated and truncated, linking to a prior item and an attached image.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a UI polish issue with the rule that headings must not duplicate or truncate.
